english: Fall seven times, stand up eight.
notes: No matter how many times you fail, keep getting back up.
Small daily efforts create great results over time.

romaji: Ishi no ue ni mo san nen
english: Even on a stone, three years.
notes: With patience and consistency, even the hardest things become possible.
Hard work always pays off, even if the result is not immediate.
Just start moving forward — new doors will appear.

japanese:
受け入れるべきものは、
ありのままに受け入れる
romaji: Ukeireru beki mono wa / ari no mama ni ukeireru
english: Accept everything just the way it is.
notes: From Dokkodo — one of the 21 precepts of the legendary samurai.
Stop worrying about past or future — focus on what you can control now.

japanese:
恐れるな、
ただ前へ進め
romaji: Osoreru na / tada mae e susume
english: Do not fear, just move forward.
notes: Fear is the only thing that truly stops us.

japanese:
勝つことより、
負けぬことを考えよ
romaji: Katsu koto yori / makenu koto o kangae yo
english: Instead of thinking about winning, think about not losing.
notes: A calm and defensive mindset often leads to greater victory.

japanese:
夢は逃げない、
逃げるのは自分だ
romaji: Yume wa nigenai / nigeru no wa jibun da
english: Dreams don't run away — the one running away is you.
notes: Popular modern motivational phrase in Japan.

japanese:
小さく始めよ、
大きく終わろう
notes: Big goals are achieved through tiny consistent steps.

japanese:
昨日より今日、
今日より明日
romaji: Kino yori kyo / kyo yori asu
english: Better today than yesterday, better tomorrow than today.
